title: End-to-end engine integration tests over InMemoryStore
purpose: Prove the engine works end to end against InMemoryStore: start records the right event and registers a discoverable handle, cancel tears down activity children and records the cancellation, and completion yields the recorded result. These are the cross-cutting tests that exercise the whole lifecycle through the public Engine API.
task: Build integration tests in crates/aion/tests that drive a real Engine over InMemoryStore using a minimal test workflow (a tiny compiled-beam fixture or a test-only module registered through the runtime) that dispatches an in-VM activity. Assert the start/register/list path, the cancel/teardown/append path, and the complete/result path. Tests run a real runtime at runtime — never gated out or #[ignore]'d.

requirements.title: Start, append, register, and list
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test that builds an Engine over InMemoryStore, starts a test workflow, and asserts WorkflowStarted is appended, the handle is registered, and list_workflows reports it.
acceptance: tests/engine_lifecycle.rs builds an Engine over a fresh InMemoryStore with one loaded test workflow.
After start_workflow, the store history's first event is WorkflowStarted with the workflow type and input.
The registry contains a Running handle for the new (WorkflowId, RunId) and list_workflows returns a matching WorkflowSummary.
The test runs under cargo test (real runtime), not gated out or ignored.

requirements.title: Cancel tears down activity child and appends cancellation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test that starts a workflow which dispatches an in-VM activity child, cancels the workflow, and asserts the activity child is no longer alive and WorkflowCancelled is appended.
acceptance: The test workflow dispatches an in-VM activity (AE-007) as a linked child that stays alive long enough to observe.
After cancel, the activity child process is no longer alive (link propagation) and the store history ends with WorkflowCancelled carrying the reason.
The registry no longer holds the handle after cancel.
The test is a real runtime test, not gated out or ignored.

requirements.title: Completion yields the recorded result
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test that starts a workflow which completes and asserts result() returns the recorded result Payload and the final history event is WorkflowCompleted.
acceptance: The test workflow runs to completion returning a known value.
result() returns the recorded result Payload equal to the workflow's output.
The store history's final event is WorkflowCompleted carrying that Payload, and list_workflows reports the workflow as Completed.
The test is a real runtime test, not gated out or ignored.

boundaries: SHALL NOT require a database — all tests use InMemoryStore (CO13).
SHALL NOT use #[ignore] or feature-gate the tests out; any runtime requirement is satisfied by the embedded beamr runtime the test builds.
SHALL NOT test AT/AD mechanics (signal routing, replay, durable timers) beyond the AE lifecycle surface — those are exercised in AT/AD.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes, including the integration tests.
Tests run a real embedded runtime against InMemoryStore with no #[ignore] and no database.
